title: Optimal n for $L^d_n$-list-colorability
statement_text: Fixing $d$, what is the optimal value of $n$ such that $[n]^d$ is $L^d_n$-list-colorable?
context_text: Theorem 1.2 establishes that $[n]^d$ is $L^d_n$-list-colorable whenever $n \geq d+2$. The paper shows this threshold is not achieved for $n < d+2$ in specific cases (e.g., $[2]^3$ is not $L^3_2$-list-colorable), making the optimal threshold an open question whose notion of optimality is justified by Proposition 3.3.
notes: Stated in running prose: 'Fixing d, the question of the optimal value of n such that [n]^d is L^d_n-list-colorable remains.' Section 5 (not fully reproduced in the extracted text) apparently contains additional formal open questions including a labeled Question 5.7 concerning whether $[n]^2$ is $\min\{L^2_n, 3\}$-list-colorable; its full statement could not be extracted.
paper_title: Mixing properties of colorings of the $\mathbb{Z}^d$ lattice
